mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, so low FEDFUNDS readings predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if lower FEDFUNDS observations do not produce higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than higher FEDFUNDS observations in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above a high-stress threshold, deteriorating labor income and recession risk dominate valuation support, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE-above-8.5 observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60 percent of cases out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales strength relative to industrial production signals final demand running ahead of current output, forcing inventory rebuilding and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth crosses above INDPRO 3-month growth
failure_condition: Reject if the RSAFS-minus-INDPRO growth interaction has nonpositive average 20-day forward returns when triggered in rolling out-of-sample evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below a depressed construction threshold signal weak household formation, impaired credit transmission, and reduced cyclical earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-700 triggered windows deliver higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than nontriggered windows in a walk-forward comparison.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, falling real policy rates increase reflation expectations and risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above FEDFUNDS by more than 2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-and-FEDFUNDS real-rate interaction has negative mean 60-day S&P 500 returns during triggered periods out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production trend signals improving capacity utilization and operating leverage, so stronger INDPRO momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher INDPRO momentum fails to rank ahead of lower INDPRO momentum in 60-day S&P 500 returns across any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll expansion combined with falling unemployment confirms broad labor-market healing, raising household income expectations and earnings forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change crosses above 0 and UNRATE 3-month change crosses below -0.2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down trigger does not produce positive 20-day returns more often than the unconditional base rate.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP growth signals aggregate-demand weakness that lowers expected revenues and risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 two-quarter growth crosses below 0 percent
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 snapshot value 13370.1 billions of chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 contraction triggers are followed by nonnegative 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60 percent of out-of-sample cases.
